Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the CRM for Abigail Clark using `crm_search_contacts` / `crm_get_contact` and retrieve her recent CRM engagement history using CRM engagement lookups. The agent needs to find Abigail's transfer-related CRM artifacts and note that her live concern in this task is attached to recent transition / communication records rather than a directly linked CRM deal for LN-2026-00008.
OE 2: Search Marcus Webb's recent email and related email threads using `search_emails` / `get_thread` with a query related to "Abigail Clark", "LN-2026-00008", or similar. The agent needs to find Abigail's direct email to Marcus asking whether he can still handle the file (email_email_62b8f4d19a01), plus Grace Yamamoto's internal email to Marcus (email_email_18f6c0b7d442) demanding a clean explanation on Abigail's file and noting that Abigail has not gotten a clean read on status from the Keystone side.
OE 3: Search Slack for Abigail's file using `conversations_search_messages` or `conversations_history` with a query related to "00008", "Clark", or similar. The agent needs to find the processing-side Slack message from Sofia Reyes in #loan-processing (C002) stating that the file is not fully clean and should not be described as "almost there" while HOI (homeowners insurance) is still open. This is the only artifact in the universe that warns against premature closing-status language on LN-2026-00008, and the agent must incorporate it when composing the reply to Abigail.
OE 4: Search the CRM engagements for the note Marcus logged after speaking with Abigail using `crm_list_engagements` or related CRM engagement lookups. The agent needs to find the engagement titled "Borrower confidence issue on LN-2026-00008" (crm_engagement_6f1d3d2c4a9b), which states that Abigail's real concern is confidence and inconsistent status communication -- she has gotten mixed answers on whether the file is close versus still waiting on items and does not feel like anyone gave her one clean update. The note makes clear the issue is not rate or product.
OE 5: Search Marcus's and Grace's recent transition-related emails and CRM records using `search_emails` plus CRM engagement lookups to identify other borrowers who recently reached out during Marcus's departure. The agent needs to identify at least the recent borrower-transfer / handoff set: Abigail Clark emailed both Marcus (email_email_62b8f4d19a01) and Grace (email_email_2109750c3562); Stephen Hamilton (email_email_91db40dac2d3) and Keisha Perry (email_email_86cfe5da7e67) emailed Grace directly. Joshua Cruz emailed Jasmine Brooks rather than Marcus or Grace (email_email_b0827782cff0) but is surfaceable through the CRM transition engagement records (crm_engagement_ab220b1ce692). The agent should discover this full borrower-transfer set through a combination of email search and CRM engagement lookups.
OE 6: Search for follow-up / response artifacts on those recent borrower contacts using `search_emails` and CRM engagements, especially for Stephen Hamilton. The agent needs to determine which borrowers are still awaiting response, which borrowers have already been contacted, and which borrowers changed position after the first transfer request. In particular, the agent needs to discover Stephen Hamilton's later retraction email (email_email_d5e8f3a20c14, subject "RE: Moving my file with Marcus Webb") in which he says his agent warned that switching companies mid-process could delay closing by weeks, asks Grace to disregard his earlier email, and states he will stay with whoever takes over from Marcus at Keystone as long as someone picks up his file quickly. Stephen should not be treated the same as borrowers still insisting on transfer.
OE 7: Search the CRM for additional transition-touchpoint notes on Abigail using `crm_list_engagements` or related CRM engagement lookups. The agent needs to find the transition outreach engagement (crm_engagement_b2d4e6f80a15, titled "Transition outreach - Clark") which records that someone at Keystone called Abigail to introduce themselves as a possible point of contact during Marcus's transition. Abigail was polite but said she would rather wait until Marcus tells her who should take over, does not want to re-explain everything to someone new, and is tired of getting different answers from different people. This note is critical context for the Abigail reply -- the agent must reflect that she has already been contacted and should not be asked to restart her story.
OE 8: Look up Danielle Webb's contact information using `crm_search_contacts` / `crm_get_contact` (and `contacts_search_contacts` if needed), then retrieve the related Danielle / Marcus file context using CRM engagements plus supporting email and Slack lookups. The agent needs to identify Danielle as buyer's agent on several Marcus purchase files (LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227 per CRM engagement crm_engagement_ec52b5bd43bd and related internal emails), recover the relevant active loan numbers from those records, and find the appraisal-gap-related file (LN-2026-00211 on Brookshire Blvd) that needs a more specific check-in.
OE 9: Search email and Slack for Danielle Webb's active-deal concerns using `search_emails`, `get_thread`, `conversations_search_messages`, or `conversations_history` with queries related to "Danielle Webb", "appraisal", "gap", "Pine Hollow", "Brookshire", or similar. The agent needs to discover Marcus's email to Danielle (email_email_77eb7a8d61d6, subject "Re: Pine Hollow appraisal came in short") showing the appraisal came back at 393 against a purchase price of 402, and that Marcus was actively working through options with Danielle to keep the deal moving. This appraisal concern should shape the content of Marcus's outreach to her rather than sending a generic transition note.
OE 10: Send an email from `marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com` to `abigail.clark@gmail.com` using `send_email`. The email should acknowledge the confusion, explicitly recognize that she has been getting mixed or conflicting answers about her file status, reflect that she has already been contacted during the transition and does not want to re-explain everything again, avoid promising any timeline or closing date Marcus cannot control (the file still has open items including homeowners insurance), and tell her he is making sure the right people have the full file context before giving her a cleaner answer.
OE 11: Send an internal summary email from `marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com` to `grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com` using `send_email`. The message should summarize Abigail's actual concern (mixed status communication and loss of confidence, not rate or product), the current borrower-transition contact picture, and the difference between borrowers still pushing for transfer (Keisha Perry still wants to continue with Marcus) versus borrowers whose concern has changed (Stephen Hamilton retracted his transfer request and now wants a prompt Keystone handoff, Abigail's core issue is communication quality not moving files).
OE 12: Send a check-in email from `marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com` to `danielle.webb@carolinahomerealty.com` using `send_email`. The email should reference her active Keystone deals (LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227) and should specifically acknowledge the appraisal issue on the Brookshire Blvd / Pine Hollow purchase (LN-2026-00211, appraisal at 393 vs 402 purchase) rather than sending a generic transition note. The email should also reassure Danielle that the transition will not leave her active deals unattended or unsupported.
OE 13: Send follow-up email responses from `marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com` to the other recent borrower contacts identified in the transition set using `send_email`. Specifically, send to Stephen Hamilton (`stephen.hamilton@gmail.com`) and Keisha Perry (`keisha.perry@icloud.com`). The response to each borrower should reflect their actual current state: Stephen Hamilton's follow-up should respect that he withdrew the transfer request and now wants a prompt Keystone handoff, and should assure him someone on the Keystone side will pick up his file quickly. Keisha Perry's follow-up should acknowledge that she asked to continue with Marcus and should provide an honest response to her transfer concern without unsupported promises.
OE 14: Log CRM complaint / concern engagements using `crm_create_engagement` for Abigail Clark, Stephen Hamilton, and Keisha Perry. Each engagement should reflect the actual borrower concern and the outreach taken: Abigail's should reference her transition / mixed-status communication concern; Stephen's should reference his current state of staying with Keystone and needing prompt handoff rather than only the original transfer request; Keisha's should reference her ongoing desire to continue with Marcus or get transfer next steps. The engagements should not treat all borrowers as having the same issue.
OE 15: Post a sales handoff update in the sales pipeline Slack channel using `conversations_add_message` (channel `C006`, `#sales-pipeline`). The message should tell the team which borrower relationships need attention during the transition and should surface the key client-relationship risks discovered in the investigation, including: Abigail Clark's mixed-communication / confidence concern, Stephen Hamilton's retracted transfer request and need for prompt Keystone-side handoff, and Keisha Perry's continued desire to follow Marcus.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email body sent to abigail.clark@gmail.com (visible in send_email parameters) acknowledges that Abigail Clark has been getting mixed, conflicting, or inconsistent answers about her file.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states: 'You told me you've been given different timelines by different people, and that no one has sat down and given you one clean, honest update on where your file actually stands.' This directly acknowledges mixed/conflicting answers.
✓Run #2The email to Abigail explicitly states: 'You've been getting different answers from different people about where your file stands, and that's not acceptable.' This directly acknowledges the mixed/conflicting answers she received.
✓Run #3The email body to Abigail explicitly states: 'You've been given different answers by different people about where your file stands, and that's not acceptable.' This clearly acknowledges the mixed/conflicting communication.

3 The email body sent to abigail.clark@gmail.com (visible in send_email parameters) states that Marcus is making sure the right people have her file's full context.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The email body states: 'I am personally making sure that the people handling your file — the processor, the loan officer assigned, and our operations lead — all have the full context of your situation and your concerns.' This directly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #2The email body states: 'I'm personally briefing the team so that no one is giving you information that conflicts with what someone else has said' and 'making sure that everyone who touches your file has the full context of where things stand.' This clearly communicates that Marcus is coordinating the handoff and ensuring full context.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'I am making sure that whoever handles your file going forward — whether that's me or someone I trust here — has the complete picture, not a version of it.' This directly states Marcus is ensuring the right people have her file's full context.
✓Run #4The email states: 'What I am going to do is personally make sure that the people handling your file have the full context of where things stand, what's still needed, and what you've already been told.' This satisfies the requirement.
✓Run #5The email to Abigail states: 'What I am doing is making sure that the people handling your file — your loan officer, your processor, and our management — all have the full context of where things stand and what you've told me.'
